title: lupinus microcarpus var. densiflorus
intro: Lupinus microcarpus var. densiflorus, also known as the dwarf or compact bush lupine, is a plant species that belongs to the lupine family and is native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and to help lower blood pressure due to its high concentration of alkaloids such as lupanine and sparteine. The health benefits of Lupinus microcarpus var. densiflorus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and possess antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, and it is also used in herbal preparations such as salves and ointments for topical application.
